title: sorbus domestica f. piriformis
intro: Sorbus domestica f. piriformis, also known as the Wild Service Tree, is a deciduous tree that belongs to the Rosaceae family, characterized by its unique, rounded or egg-shaped fruit. The medicinal properties of Sorbus domestica f. piriformis lie in its bark, which contains tannins and other bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and protect against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the bark of Sorbus domestica f. piriformis is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
